ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and welcome the message. They produce
text: Na nea beguu no asaase pa mu he gyina hɔ ma nnipa a bɛte asɛm he a bɛgye na bɛsõ aba mmɔho aduasa ne aduosia ne mmɔho ha.” Kanea a ɔhyɛ Atam Ase
translated: And he came to a good land and stood there for people who were discussing and he listened and took thirty-six of their proverbs.

ref: As soon as Jesus came out of the water, he saw the sky open and the Holy Spirit coming down to him like a dove.
text: Na oofiri nsuo he mu apie heaa, ohuu soro sɛ abie a na Honhom he oosiane sɛ abuburo ɔɔba ne so.
translated: When the water in the river dries up, you see stones that were under the water, just like when Honhom's anger subsides, you see the wickedness in his heart.

ref: Jesus told the man to stand up where everyone could see him.
text: Na ɔka kyerɛɛ nea ne nsa adwodwo he sɛ, “Bra ha!”
translated: And he showed him his hand that was wounded and said, "Come here!"

ref: Six days later Jesus took Peter and the brothers James and John with him. They went up on a very high mountain where they could be alone.
text: Nnansia akyi he, Yesu faa Petro ne Yakobo ne ne nua Yohane kaa ne ho kɔɔ bepɔ tentene bi so.
translated: Six days later, Jesus took Peter and James and his brother John and led them up a high mountain by themselves.

ref: So when you go to a town or a village, find someone able and willing to have you as their guest and stay with them until you leave.
text: Kuro anaa akuraa biaa a hobɛkɔ mu he, hommisa deɛ ɔfata a ɔya hɔ, na hontena hɔ kosi sɛ hobefiri hɔ.
translated: The elder who is in the house when a child is born, if he is worthy, he names the child, and the name stays until the child grows up.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Berɛ a oduruu Yerusalem he, kuro mũ he nyinaa bɔɔ twi, bisaa sɛ, “Hwane di?”
translated: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred and asked, "Who is this?"

ref: After the apostles returned to Jesus, they told him everything they had done and taught.
text: Afei asomafoɔ he twaa Yesu ho hyiaeɛ, na bɛkaa nea bɛayɔ ne nea bɛakyerɛkyerɛ nyinaa kyerɛɛ no.
translated: The chief priests and the whole Sanhedrin were looking for evidence against Jesus so that they could put him to death, but they did not find any.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Dua biaa a ɔnso aba pa he betwa to twene gya mu.
translated: The child's mother has also come to the village to buy yams.

ref: “You know two days from now will be Passover. This is when the Son of Man will be handed over to his enemies and nailed to a cross.”
text: “Honim sɛ, aaka nnanu na Twam afahyɛ he aduru, na bebeyi Nipa Ba he ama, na bɛabɔ no asennua mu.”
translated: They say that, Grandmother went to Twam festival at Aduru, and they gave Nipa Ba to her, and they bathed her in the river.

ref: Don't you know that the food you put into your mouth goes into your stomach and then out of your body?
text: Honnim sɛ bibiaa a ɔkɔ anum he kɔ yafunum na opie kɔ yaaneem?
translated: What kind of woman goes to the market and buys pepper to go and throw it away?

ref: Jesus did not let anyone go with him except Peter and the two brothers, James and John.
text: Wamma biaa anni n’akyi gye Petro, Yakubu ne Yohane, Yakubu nua he.
translated: Then he saw Peter, James, and John, James' brother.

ref: But you prove you really are the relatives of the ones who killed the prophets.
text: Nti hodi adanseɛ tia ho ho sɛ hoyɛ bɛ a bekuku adiyifoɔ he asefoɔ.
translated: The chief of Adanse has gone to the forest to search for the lost hunter and his dogs.

ref: The seeds that fell on good ground are the people who hear and welcome the message. They produce
text: Na nea beguu no asaase pa mu he gyina hɔ ma nnipa a bɛte asɛm he a bɛgye na bɛsõ aba mmɔho aduasa ne aduosia ne mmɔho ha.” Kanea a ɔhyɛ Atam Ase
translated: And he came to a good land and stood there for people who were discussing and he listened and took thirty-six of their proverbs.

ref: Peter replied, “Remember, we have left everything to be your followers! What will we get?”
text: Na afei Petro yii ano sɛ, “Hwɛ, yɛagya nnoɔma nyinaa hɔ abedi w’akyi, mmɛ ne yebenya?”
translated: And Peter said to them, "Look, we have left everything to follow you, what shall we have?"

ref: to ask Jesus, “Are you the one we should be looking for? Or must we wait for someone else?”
text: ma bɛ bebisaa Yesu sɛ, “Wone deɛ ɔɔba he, anaa yɛnhwɛ foforɔ kwane?”
translated: They asked Jesus, "Where is the king, or should we prepare another place?"

ref: Jesus replied, “It isn't right to take food away from children and feed it to dogs.”
text: Na Yesu buaa sɛ, “Ɔnyɛ fɛ sɛ bɛfa mma aduane to ma nkramane mma.”
translated: Jesus said, "It is not right to take the children's bread and toss it to the dogs."

ref: You have heard people say, “Love your neighbors and hate your enemies.”
text: “Hoate sɛ bɛkaa sɛ, ‘Dɔ wo yɔnko na tane w’atamfoɔ.’
translated: They say that the elders say, 'Love your neighbor and respect your enemies.'

ref: I promise you that any of the sinful things you say or do can be forgiven, no matter how terrible those things are.
text: Nɔkorɛ, meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, ɔde nipa bɔne ne abususɛm biaa a ɔbɛka bɛkyɛ no,
translated: Now, I will tell you that, he brought all the bad people and all the stories that he would say and share,

ref: In fact, he has already come. But the people did not recognize him and treated him just as they wanted to. They will soon make the Son of Man suffer in the same way.”
text: nso meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ Elia aba dada, na bɛanhu no, na bɛyɔɔ no deɛ bɛpɛ biaa. Saa ara nso ne ɔnkyɛre biaa, Nipa Ba he nso behu amane bɛ nsam.”
translated: They said that Elijah had come back, and they saw him, and they did to him whatever they wanted. Even so, he did not get angry, the Son of Man also saw and kept quiet.

ref: Every tree producing bad fruit will be chopped down and burned.
text: Dua biaa a ɔnso aba pa he betwa to twene gya mu.
translated: The child's mother has also come to the village to buy yams.

ref: When Jesus came to Jerusalem, everyone in the city was excited and asked, “Who can this be?”
text: Berɛ a oduruu Yerusalem he, kuro mũ he nyinaa bɔɔ twi, bisaa sɛ, “Hwane di?”
translated: When he entered Jerusalem, the whole city was stirred and asked, "Who is this?"

ref: So they left the crowd, and his disciples started across the lake with him in the boat. Some other boats followed along.
text: Nti begyaa nnipadɔm he kwane wieeɛ he, bɛde Yesu tenaa kodoɔ he mu kɔeɛ; na akodoɔ foforɔ nso ka ho. Na akodoɔ binom ka ne ho.
translated: The next day the crowd that had stayed on the opposite shore of the lake realized that only one boat had been there, and that Jesus had not entered it with his disciples, but that they had gone away alone.

ref: where they stayed until Herod died. So the Lord's promise came true, just as the prophet had said, “I called my son out of Egypt.” The Killing of the Children
text: na bɛtenaa hɔ kosii sɛ Herode wuiɛ. Yei maa deɛ Awurade nam diyifoɔ he so kaeɛ he baa mu sɛ, “Misraim ne mafrɛ me ba he afiri.” Bekuu mmɔfra
translated: An angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream and said, "Get up, take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt." After Herod died

ref: But the ones who should have been in the kingdom will be thrown out into the dark. They will cry and grit their teeth in pain.”
text: na ahennie he mma paa deɛ bɛbɛto bɛ atwene sum kabii mu, baabi a suu ne benabɔ ya.”
translated: They said that when they were going to the forest to cut trees, some people heard the voices of dwarfs.

ref: So the demons begged Jesus, “If you force us out, please send us into those pigs!”
text: Na ahohommɔne he srɛɛ no sɛ, “Sɛ wotutu yɛ a, ma yɛnkɔ mprakokuo yi mu.”
translated: The old woman said to him, "If you are going to farm, let us go to this forest."

ref: But I tell you not to swear by anything when you make a promise! Heaven is God's throne, so don't swear by heaven.
text: Na me deɛ meeka akyerɛ ho sɛ, Nka ntam koraa, mfa soro nka ntam, efiri sɛ ɔyɛ Nyankopɔn ahennwa,
translated: And I also want to explain that, Do not swear at all, do not swear heaven, because He is God's throne

ref: Jesus went with Jairus. Many people followed along and kept crowding around.
text: Nti ɔne no kɔeɛ. Na nnipadɔm kɛseɛ dii n’akyi, na bɛkyeree ne so.
translated: He went to the bush. A large crowd followed him, and they surrounded him.

ref: Jesus said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? Only God is good. If you want to have eternal life, you must obey his commandments.”
text: Na obisaa no sɛ, ‘Mmɛ nti ne wobisa me papa ho asɛm? Baako pɛ ne yɛ papa. Sɛ wopɛ sɛ wo nya nkwa a, di ahyɛdeɛ he so.”
translated: When his father asked him, "Why do you ask me about the matter of my father?" One of them is your father. If you want to get life, eat what is on the stool.

ref: One Sabbath Jesus and his disciples were walking through some wheat fields. His disciples were picking grains of wheat as they went along.
text: Na ɔbaa sɛ homeda bi na Yesu nam aburofuo bi mu, na bɛɛkɔ he, n’asuafoɔ he hyɛɛ aseɛ pempanee aburo mmɛtem he bi.
translated: And a woman who had a flow of blood for twelve years came from behind and touched the hem of Jesus' garment, and immediately her bleeding stopped.

ref: But when he heard that Herod's son Archelaus was now ruler of Judea, he was afraid to go there. Then in a dream he was told to go to Galilee,
text: Nso ɔtee sɛ Arkelao asi ne papa Herode anane mu oodi adeɛ Yudea he, osuroo sɛ ɔbɛkɔ hɔ. Na afei, bɛbɔɔ no kɔkɔ daeɛ mu he, ɔkɔɔ Galilea mansinim.
translated: When he heard that Archelaus was reigning over Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Then, after being warned in a dream, he went to the regions of Galilee.
